member_narrative:
Mother Lovie's Family Tree began growing when she was born August 18, 1890, in Cherokee County, South Carolina to her parents, Nelson and Lovamae Lipscomb.


Although slavery had been abolished many years earlier, Negros still experienced the harsh reality of racism, inequalitiy, and racial segregation brought about by Jim Crow Laws.  This triggered the Great Migration of Negros seeking equality and a better way of life, migrating from southern states, like the Carolina's, to northern states like Ohio and Illinois.  Many of Mother Lovie's children would join this great migration.


Mother Lovie met the love of her life, Acie Watkins, who lived just accross the South Carolina border, in nearby Forest City, North Carolina.  On October 5, 1902, Mother Lovie and Acie Watkins were married.  Taking on her husband's surname, and moving with him to Forest City, North Carolina, Lovie Lipscomb became Lovie Watkins.


Over the years, Mother Lovie and Acie Watkins's Family Tree would sprout nineteen branches, one branch for each of their 19 children.  Of course, their children, themselves, would sprout branches of their own bringing forth Mother Lovie's and Acie Watkins's grand-children, great-grand-children, and great-great-grand-children.


In the year 1930, at the time of the birth of their 19th and final child, Perry Watkins (and including him), thirteen of Mother Lovie and Acie Watkins's nineteen children were born alive and still living.  Four others were born alive but were deceased, and one other was stillborn.  At the time of this documentary, the names and birth sequences of the four deceased and the one stillborn were ambiguous.  This documentary substitutes unknown in lieu of first names for these children.


The clickable names of Mother Lovie and Acie Watkins’s children are displayed below.  Like a video game where you click or tap an emoji, to reveal a prize hidden underneath; click or tap the names of Mother Lovie and Acie Watkins's children to find out more about them.  If more information is available, it will be displayed.

You’ll also want to click the Family Tree Birthdays button (at the bottom of the page) to view Mother Lovie's and Acie Watkins's descendant Birthdays by Month.



Sincerely,

Christine Scott
Granddaughter
(Seventh Child of Mother Lovie's 19th child)
Perry Watkins

member_descendants.member_narrative:
Born February 23, 1930, in Forest City, North Carolina.  Perry is the 19th and final child of Mother Lovie and Acie Watkins.  Perry met his wife to be, Elizabeth Collins, who lived just accross the North Carolina border, in nearby Spartanburg, South Carolina.  On October 29th, 1949, Perry and Elizabeth were married.


Perry and his brothers Marshall, Robert  (Bubba Lee), and Wayne; and their two sisters, Orlene and Pauline, were among the Great Migration of Negros seeking equality and a better way of life, migrating from southern states, like the Carolina's, to northern states like Ohio and Illinois.


Before migrating up north, Perry and Elizabeth's first four children (John, Elizabeth Irene, Patrica, and Loretta) were born in Rutherford County, Forest City, North Carolina.  After migrating up north, their second four children (Joseph, Jerry, Christine, and Rejeana) were born in Cook County, Chicago, Illinois.


Perry and his wife Elizabeth's Family Tree sprouted eight branches, one branch for each of their 8 children.  Of course, their children, themselves, would sprout branches of their own bringing forth Perry and Elizabeth's grand-children, great-grand-children, and great-great-grand-children.


Explore Perry and Elizabeth's Family Tree by clicking or tapping the names of their children to find out more about them.  If more information is available, it will be displayed.  You’ll also want to click the Family Tree Birthdays button (at the bottom of the page) to view Perry and Elizabeth's descendant Birthdays by Month.


Sincerely,

Christine Scott

Perry and Elizabeth Watkins'
7th Born Child (a.k.a. Tina)

Mostly known to the family as Tina, only brother Jerry calls her Christine . . .

A Microsoft Office 365 Data Management Specialist.  Who also enjoys doing Graphic Design and Digital Imagery, Jewelry Design and Jewelry Creation projects too!  A dedicated Christian and follower of Jesus Christ.

A strong advocate and supporter for the development of children and loves to sing.

May 9, 2020, Gone, but never ever forgotten . . . Happy Birthday baby, until we meet again . . .
Love Mama
Our 2nd grandson, Daraven was almost born in Cancun Mexico.
